cot_content:
Okay, let's think through this question. The goal is to figure out how to tell right and left earbuds apart without looking. The options are about tying a knot on either the speaker end or the connecting end of the left earbud.

First, I need to understand the parts of an earbud. An earbud typically has two main parts: the speaker part that goes into the ear and the connecting cable that links it to the other earbud and the device. So when they say "speaker end," they're referring to the part that goes into the ear. The "connecting end" would be the part of the cable that connects to the other earbud or the main cord.

Now, the question is about distinguishing between the left and right earbuds. If you tie a knot at the speaker end of the left earbud (option A), when you put the earbuds in your ears, the knot would be on the left side. This could help you identify which one is the left earbud by feel. However, if you tie the knot at the connecting end (option B), the knot would be on the cable, but since the cable is the same on both sides, it might not be as helpful when trying to identify the earbuds by touch when they're in your ears.

The key is that when the earbuds are in your ears, you can feel the speaker end but not the connecting end. So tying a knot at the speaker end (option A) would allow you to identify the left earbud by touch, whereas tying a knot at the connecting end (option B) would make it harder to distinguish since you can't feel that part when the earbud is in your ear.

Therefore, the correct answer should be A.
label: majority
